No malicious behavior detected.\n- **Conclusion:** The high obfuscated score is questionable. It should be 0, as the code is not intentionally obfuscated maliciously, just minified.\n- **Scores:** malware=0, securityRisk=0.1 (a slight concern due to obfuscation), confidence=1.\n\n**Issue:** The obfuscated score seems unjustified; the code is standard, just minified. The securityRisk score of 0.1 is also somewhat high for a simple factory class.\n\n---\n\n### **Report 4**\n- **Purpose & Sources:** Correct.\n- **Sinks & Flows:** No untrusted data.\n- **Anomalies & Analysis:** Marked as \"obfuscated\" (obfuscated=1), but again, the code is a straightforward, autogenerated factory. This overstates obfuscation, which is not malicious but just minification.\n\n- **Risk score:** Given the nature of the code, the risk is minimal. Slightly elevated in report 3 due to obfuscation flag, but overall, the code is safe.\n\n---\n\n### **My final evaluation:**\n\n- **Malware score:** 0 (no signs of malware)\n- **Obfuscated score:** 0 (no malicious obfuscation; just minified/generated code)\n- **Risk score:** 0.1 (very low, standard boilerplate code, no vulnerabilities)\n\n**Conclusion:** The code is a typical, autogenerated, safe contract factory with no malware, no obfuscation intended to hide malicious behavior, and negligible security risk.\n\n---\n\n**Note:** The overestimation of obfuscation in some reports should be corrected to 0, as minification does not equate to obfuscation or malicious intent.",
